question: Looking at the median income map, which statement below is the most accurate?

options.A: California and Delaware likely belong to very different income levels.
options.B: California and Delaware have similar median incomes.
options.C: The median income differences between the highlighted states are impossible to determine from this visualization.
options.D: The median income gap between California and Delaware might exceeds that between Virginia and Utah.

explanation: California (50.5$k) and Delaware (57.5$k) have a 7.0$k median income difference, while Virginia (39.6$k) and Utah (40.2$k) have only a 0.6$k difference. However, the categorical color encoding groups California and Delaware into the same color bin (50-60$k range), making their large difference appear similar, while Virginia and Utah appear more different due to different color bins.
